evals.prompt: How do I share a database session and avoid creating a new connection for every Depends() call in FastAPI?
evals.expected_output: FastAPI caches Depends() results within the same request scope by default. Define get_db as an async generator yielding the session. Any dependency that calls Depends(get_db) within the same request reuses the same session instance. This is automatic — no extra configuration needed.

evals.prompt: I'm using @app.on_event('startup') to initialize my database. Is this the right approach?
evals.expected_output: No. @app.on_event is deprecated. Use the lifespan context manager instead: define an async function decorated with @asynccontextmanager that yields, and pass it to FastAPI(lifespan=lifespan). Code before yield runs on startup, code after yield runs on shutdown.

evals.prompt: How do I create a reusable dependency for the currently authenticated user that can be used across multiple routers?
evals.expected_output: Define get_current_user as an async dependency function that extracts and verifies the JWT token. Then create a type alias using Annotated: CurrentUser = Annotated[User, Depends(get_current_user)]. Use this alias as a parameter type in any route function. FastAPI will inject the dependency automatically.

should_not_trigger: How do I write a table-driven test for a Python validation function?
